mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the process of running Kubernetes on AWS. It provides a highly available and secure Kubernetes environment, but may incur costs based on AWS resources used. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS infrastructure and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services, including EKS.

```bash

curl "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/AWSCLIV2.pkg" -o "AWSCLIV2.pkg"

sudo installer -pkg AWSCLIV2.pkg -target /

aws --version

```

> Download the AWS CLI package.
> Install the AWS CLI.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new EKS cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID and subnet IDs with your actual values.

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the new EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This sets up your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks if the nodes in the EKS cluster are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Set Up Monitoring and Autoscaling
steps.description:
This step configures monitoring and autoscaling for your EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler-template.yaml

```

> Apply the Cluster Autoscaler configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running EKS incurs costs based on the AWS resources you provision.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will delete the EKS cluster and all associated resources, leading to data loss.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

```

> This will remove the cluster and its resources.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Check for and manually delete any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers

```

> List load balancers to identify orphaned ones.

upgrade.title: Upgrade EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide > backup-nodes.txt

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current node information.
> Upgrade the cluster to the new version.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the EKS cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Cluster not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified cluster name does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster name and ensure it was created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the nodes are still initializing or there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs and ensure there are sufficient resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Access denied
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the IAM role does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the IAM role associated with the EKS cluster has the required permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features and enhancements.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain APIs that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the migration guide for any breaking changes when upgrading from 1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and AWS account permissions.
